summary: Draft PR for menu data-delete decomp work in src/melee/mn/mndatadel, adding bodies for several previously stubbed mnDataDel/fn_8024xxxx routines, refining mndatadel data structs and prototypes, and documenting MenuInputState fields. Bot report showed two function matches and one .bss match plus multiple partial function improvements, with one .sdata2 regression.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: adds includes, local gm declarations, bodies for mnDataDel_8024E940, mnDataDel_8024EA6C, mnDataDel_8024EBC8, fn_8024F1D4, fn_8024F318, fn_8024F840, fn_8024FBA4, fn_8024FC48, fn_8024FD40, mnDataDel_8024FE4C, mnDataDel_80250170, and improves fn_8024ECCC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl:bot report new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Public prototypes updated from UNK_PARAMS or anonymous HSD_GObj pointers to typed signatures; adds baselib/jobj.h include for HSD_JObj.
Static data/type recovery: adds MnDataDelSelectData, MnDataDelUserData, comments for WarnCmnData, arrays in MnDataDelData, SetItemChildAnim inline, and extern float/static model declarations.
Adds field comments for MenuInputState cooldown, x2, and x4.

what_changed.text: Replaced many mndatadel stubs with C bodies for menu deletion, warning-modal flow, menu input handling, item deletion animation, teardown, and setup paths.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:mndatadel.c removed /// # stubs and added function bodies
text_corpus.jsonl:bot report lists 3 new matches and 11 improvements

what_changed.text: Added recovered menu userdata and selection structs, converted repeated scalar fields in MnDataDelData into arrays for menu joint indices and SIS label IDs.
Updated mndatadel prototypes to include HSD_JObj and named HSD_GObj parameters, replacing one UNK_PARAMS declaration for mnDataDel_8024EBC8.
Added comments to MenuInputState fields in mnmain.h, describing cooldown and reset fields.

smart_moves.text: Used typed menu data structures and arrays for repeated menu entries, which makes repeated menu item operations indexable rather than keeping only separate scalar fields.
Header prototypes were updated alongside recovered function bodies, reducing UNK_PARAMS and making call surfaces more truthful.
The PR kept address-style names for many not-yet-understood functions and globals, avoiding unsupported semantic renames.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When menu data has repeated item entries, recovering array fields in the static data struct can enable compact indexed loops for animation and text-label handling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:mndatadel.static.h converts x3C/x58 scalar series to arrays
raw_diff:mndatadel.c fn_8024F840 indexes menu_data->x3C and x58

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovered function signatures should be reflected in the owning header when bodies prove argument types, as shown by replacing mnDataDel_8024EBC8(UNK_PARAMS) with HSD_JObj* and u8 arguments.

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses local-role pointer names such as gobj, child, panel, exclaim, cursor_yes, cursor_no, menu_data, user_data, and data_tbl where roles are apparent.
Unproven functions and globals largely remain address-style names such as fn_8024F318, mnDataDel_804DC1A8, gm_801647D0, and lb_80011E24.

Recovered struct fields mix semantic names for clearer fields, such as selected_item, confirm_active, item_deleted, label_text, menu_joints, visible, and cursor_idx, with xN names for less-certain fields.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Uses explicit stack padding locals in several functions, e.g. u8 _pad arrays, likely to shape stack layout; the PR evidence does not include objdiff reasoning for each padding use.
Preserves an unused argument in mnDataDel_8024EBC8 with '(void) arg3; // unused but affects caller register allocation', explicitly marking a register-allocation tactic.
Introduces natural loops over menu items and stage/item slots, including do-while loops and for loops over six menu entries.
Uses direct gobj->hsd_obj access in fn_8024ECCC instead of GET_JOBJ; the PR evidence shows the change but does not explain whether this was required for matching.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported matched code +0.02% (+672 bytes), matched data +48 bytes, 3 new matches: mnDataDel_80250170, fn_8024F1D4, and .bss.
Bot also reported 11 improvements in unmatched mndatadel items, including fn_8024F318 to 89.17%, mnDataDel_8024FE4C to 95.67%, fn_8024F840 to 83.19%, and fn_8024ECCC to 97.03%.
Bot reported one unmatched-function regression: main/melee/mn/mndatadel .sdata2 -3 bytes, 42.11% to 36.36%.
No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: mnDataDel_8024EBC8 unused arg3 affects caller register allocation
mndatadel x3C x58 arrays menu joint indices SIS label IDs
gm_1601 function prototypes gm_801647D0 gm_801641CC gm_80164430 gm_80164250 gm_801641E4
mndatadel local declarations correct signatures owning header

notes: PR body is empty and no human review comments are present.
Diff evidence is strong for changed files, functions touched, prototype/type changes, and visible tactics.
Bot report provides match/improvement/regression numbers but only for one reported comparison; no local build logs, objdiff details, or author rationale are present.
PR is closed with merged_at null and marked draft in raw PR JSON; do not treat these changes as accepted into the project.

Some matching tactics such as stack padding, unused arg preservation, direct gobj->hsd_obj access, and local function declarations are visible but not justified by detailed verification evidence in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover repeated menu data as arrays when indexed menu code supports it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In mndatadel, repeated menu joint and SIS label fields were modeled as arrays x3C[7] and x58[6], enabling loops and indexed selected_item handling across six menu entries.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update public prototypes when recovering mndatadel function signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR replaced mnDataDel_8024EBC8(UNK_PARAMS) with a typed HSD_JObj*/u8 signature and named several HSD_GObj* parameters after adding function bodies.

source_update_candidates.title: Local declarations for recovered external functions should be reviewed against owning headers
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2078 adds source-local declarations for gm_1601 functions with a comment saying they have correct signatures. This may be a useful example for the truthful-headers standard: if these signatures are proven, curator should consider whether the owning gm header should declare them rather than keeping local declarations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:mndatadel.c comment 'Local declarations for gm_1601 functions with correct signatures'
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports that local declarations were added, but not whether owning headers existed or were intentionally avoided.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit evidence for unused-argument register-allocation tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2078 contains '(void) arg3; // unused but affects caller register allocation' in mnDataDel_8024EBC8. This is a concrete historical example of a matching tactic that should remain narrow and evidence-backed.
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded evidence shows the tactic and author comment, but does not include objdiff proof for curator acceptance.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR as merged or accepted; metadata shows CLOSED with merged_at null.
Do not claim all added functions matched; bot report shows only mnDataDel_80250170 and fn_8024F1D4 as new function matches, with many other functions still partial.
Do not infer human reviewer intent; no human review comments are present.
Do not accept stack padding, direct GET_JOBJ replacement, local gm declarations, or .sdata2 changes as standards from this slice alone.
